title: Hash Layers For Large Sparse Models
abstract: This paper introduces a simple, sparse, and efficient routing strategy for Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) models, called Hash Layers. The method routes input tokens to fixed expert modules using a hash function. It requires no extra parameters, no changes to the objective function, and is presented as a robust, fast, and easy-to-implement alternative to existing methods like Switch Transformers and BASE Layers, particularly for training very large models with limited compute budgets.

methodology.problem: Routing (gating) in MoE models can be computationally expensive or lead to issues like expert imbalance or over-specialization. Existing sparse methods like Switch Transformers require load balancing terms, and BASE Layers use complex assignment algorithms.
methodology.solution: A new routing strategy called 'Hash Layers'.
methodology.mechanism: Tokens are routed to fixed expert modules based on a hash of their input representation. This is applied by replacing the standard Feed-Forward Network (FFN) in a Transformer with an MoE version.
key_characteristics: No change to objective function or assignment algorithm

claims: The Hash Layer approach is effective on a number of datasets.
It compares favorably to Switch Transformers and BASE Layers.
It is advocated as a strong candidate for training very large models with limited compute budgets ('one shot').
